critics.name: General Editor
critics.notes: Excellent structure: cold open in the Hwaseong showroom, logical progression through architecture inversion, Katerra/Veev counterpoint, cost decomposition, design critique, buyer implications. Engagement is strong throughout. The counterargument section ('this criticism is romantic...') is one of the best I've seen — gives the opposing view full volume. Word count at ~2,034 exceeds the 1,200 deep-dive guideline but the content earns it; the cost decomposition and architectural criticism are substantive enough that cutting would lose original contribution. Minor: the limitations paragraph at the end could be tightened by 30% without losing substance.

critics.name: Voice Coach
critics.notes: Elena Vasquez voice is well-captured: elegant, spatial, essayistic. 'The bedroom features an air conditioner calibrated by Samsung's AI to the room's exact cubic volume' — she sees the building as art before technology. Sentence rhythm: variance 458 (target ≥200), short 11.4% (target ≤15%), long 58.6% (target ≥15%) — all pass. No banned phrases. No 'The' starters. Em dash count: 1 (≤3). The 'like a portrait where the eyes are a millimeter too close together' comparison is exactly her register.

critics.name: Ethics Reviewer
critics.notes: No self-congratulation. Balanced treatment: Samsung/LG get genuine credit for manufacturing discipline AND genuine criticism for design inversion. Katerra/Veev comparison is fair — acknowledges 'capital structure and governance failures alongside operational ones.' The strongest counterargument is not strawmanned. No moral preachiness. The architectural critique is aesthetic, not moralistic.

critics.name: Social/Shareability
critics.notes: Strong headline with the decade-long rivalry framing. Two stat callouts work as social cards ($248/sq ft, $2B+ Katerra). The 'rooms serve it; bedrooms happen to exist nearby' line is highly quotable. 'If SmartThings deprecates a protocol, your walls are the ones that notice' is a perfect tweet-length close. The Katerra comparison provides the tension that drives shares. Minor: could use one more visual pull-quote in the 'What Gets Lost' section.

critics.name: Legal Accuracy
critics.notes: All factual claims linked to sources: Seoul Economic Daily, Prefabium, KED Global, Mordor Intelligence, Korea Biz Wire, Not Boring (Katerra). No unsubstantiated legal claims. Katerra Chapter 11 filing is a matter of public record. Veev shutdown is documented. No liability-creating advice. The 'not a recommendation' posture is implicit and appropriate for editorial journalism.

critics.name: Research Rigor
critics.notes: Original contribution: cost decomposition of LG MONO Core 27 showing ~13% appliance share at ~$9,600 of $72,000 total, yielding ~$215/sq ft structure-only. This appears to be novel analysis — no other source breaks out the appliance vs. structure cost. Limitations section is thorough and honest: Korean pricing caveat, transfer pricing unknown, no post-occupancy data, no customer satisfaction data, U.S. infrastructure doesn't exist. Strongest counterargument engaged at full strength. Methodology transparent: shows appliance-by-appliance price estimates. Verifiability: all claims traceable to linked sources.

critics.name: Data Presentation
critics.notes: Stat callouts are clear and well-chosen ($248/sq ft total, $2B+ Katerra context). Cost decomposition in body text is transparent with line-item breakdowns. US Census Bureau comparison contextualizes the $215/sq ft figure. 'Space Factory' in the limitations paragraph is actually 'Gonggan Jejakso' (literally 'Space Factory' in Korean) — should use the Korean name for consistency or clarify the translation. Market sizing (Mordor Intelligence $41.45B) properly attributed. Minor: the 42.45% residential share stat could use a source link.

revision_suggestions: Consider tightening the limitations paragraph — it's comprehensive but could be 30% shorter without losing substance
Clarify 'Space Factory' = Gonggan Jejakso (공간 제작소) for reader consistency
42.45% residential share claim needs direct source link
